questions.statement: The Allahabad pillar inscription of Samudragupta was composed by ______.

Which region in India has the highest concentration of Iron and Steel Industry?
Who called dams as the &lsquo;Temples of Modern India&rsquo;?
Which of the following Buddhist texts is often called the &#39;Gita of Buddhism&#39; for its importance and teachings?

If an object of mass 2 kg is dropped from a height of 10 metres, what will be the ratio of its potential energy and kinetic energy at the height of 5 metres (g = 10m/sec<sup>2</sup> )
Who was the first Indian musician to be awarded the Bharat Ratna, India&rsquo;s highest civilian honor?
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is M.S. Subbulakshmi.

Key Points

  • M.S. Subbulakshmi was the first Indian musician to be awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honor, in 1998.
  • She was a renowned Carnatic vocalist, whose contributions to Indian classical music earned her unparalleled respect and recognition globally.
  • Known as the "Queen of Carnatic Music," her performances captivated audiences, blending traditional and devotional music.
  • She was the first Indian musician to perform at the United Nations General Assembly in 1966, showcasing the global appeal of Indian classical music.
  • Her renditions of devotional songs, such as "Bhaja Govindam" and "Vishnu Sahasranamam," are widely celebrated and continue to inspire musicians and audiences alike.

Additional Information

  • Bharat Ratna:
    • The Bharat Ratna is India's highest civilian award, instituted in 1954, to recognize exceptional service in arts, literature, public service, and science.
    • It is awarded without distinction of race, occupation, position, or gender and is bestowed by the President of India.
    • The award consists of a peepal leaf-shaped medal and a certificate signed by the President of India.
    • As of October 2023, 48 individuals have received this prestigious honor.
  • M.S. Subbulakshmi's Background:
    • Born on 16 September 1916, in Madurai, Tamil Nadu, Subbulakshmi was trained in Carnatic music from a young age.
    • She was mentored by her mother, Shanmukhavadivu, a veena player, and honed her skills under the guidance of Semmangudi Srinivasa Iyer.
    • She was also an accomplished actress, appearing in films like "Meera" (1945), which popularized her devotional songs.
  • Other Bharat Ratna Awardees in Music:
    • Pandit Ravi Shankar: Renowned sitar virtuoso, awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1999.
    • Ustad Bismillah Khan: Legendary shehnai player, honored with the Bharat Ratna in 2001.
  • Significance of Carnatic Music:
    • Carnatic music is a form of Indian classical music that originated in the southern states of India.
    • It is characterized by intricate compositions, devotional themes, and the use of ragas and talas.
    • M.S. Subbulakshmi's contributions played a pivotal role in popularizing Carnatic music on a global stage.

Important Points

  • Ustad Bismillah Khan: Known for revolutionizing the shehnai as a classical instrument, awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2001.
  • Pandit Ravi Shankar: Famous for his sitar performances and collaborations with international musicians, awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1999.
  • Alka Yagnik: A playback singer known for her contributions to Bollywood, but she has not received the Bharat Ratna.
